question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take / IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy a market with an IEnterGate whose canIncreaseCredit implementation burns near-block-gas-limit in a storage loop trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys GasGriefGate -> touchMarket(market with enterGate=GasGriefGate) -> victim taker calls take() with offer.buy=true causing buyerCreditIncrease > 0, violating the invariant that any valid take with credit increase must complete within a single block gas budget, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS of all credit-increasing takes in the gated market? Proof idea: deploy GasGriefGate.canIncreaseCredit that loops over a large storage array; fuzz loop length and assert take() reverts with out-of-gas before position updates at Midnight.sol:408-410.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take / IEnterGate.canIncreaseDebt] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy a market with an IEnterGate whose canIncreaseDebt implementation performs an unbounded SLOAD loop trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys GasGriefGate -> touchMarket(market with enterGate=GasGriefGate) -> victim seller calls take() with sellerDebtIncrease > 0, violating the invariant that any valid debt-increasing take must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS of all new borrowing in the gated market? Proof idea: fuzz the gate's internal storage array length from 1 to 10000 and measure gas consumed by take(); assert that at some threshold take() becomes unexecutable in a single block.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidatorGate.canLiquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy a market with an ILiquidatorGate whose canLiquidate implementation iterates over a growing approved-liquidator list trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys GrowingListGate -> touchMarket(market with liquidatorGate=GrowingListGate) -> borrower becomes unhealthy -> liquidator calls liquidate(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where undercollateralized debt cannot be liquidated and bad debt accumulates? Proof idea: fuzz the approved-liquidator list length; assert that liquidate() reverts OOG before the bitmap loop at Midnight.sol:607 when list length exceeds threshold.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidatorGate.canLiquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy a market with an ILiquidatorGate that reverts after consuming 29M gas trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys RevertAfterGasGate -> touchMarket -> borrower becomes unhealthy -> any liquidator calls liquidate(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent liquidation DoS enabling bad debt accumulation without any lender recourse? Proof idea: deploy gate that burns gas then reverts; assert liquidate() always reverts with LiquidatorGatedFromLiquidating and that no alternative liquidation path exists in the protocol.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy / collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) collaterals each backed by a gas-heavy IOracle trigger the call sequence borrower calls supplyCollateral() 16 times across 16 different collateral indices -> borrower takes a sell offer to create debt -> any caller calls isHealthy(market, id, borrower), violating the invariant that health checks must complete within reasonable gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing of every take() call where the seller has 16 activated collaterals since isHealthy is called at Midnight.sol:476? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles each consuming 100k gas in price(); fuzz collateral count from 1 to 16 and measure gas cost of take() at the final health check.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate 16 collaterals each with a gas-heavy oracle and then become unhealthy trigger the call sequence borrower activates 16 collaterals via supplyCollateral() -> price drops -> liquidator calls liquidate(), violating the invariant that liquidation of unhealthy positions must be executable, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the bitmap loop at Midnight.sol:607-618 calls IOracle.price() 16 times making liquidation prohibitively expensive or impossible in a single block? Proof idea: fuzz oracle gas cost per call from 10k to 200k; assert total liquidate() gas stays under block limit for all valid collateral counts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket / collateralParams for-loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they construct a Market with collateralParams.length == MAX_COLLATERALS (128) trigger the call sequence market_creator calls touchMarket(market with 128 CollateralParams), violating the invariant that market creation must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the for-loop at Midnight.sol:762-773 iterates 128 times making market creation or first-touch of any entry point prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: fuzz collateralParams.length from 1 to 128 and measure touchMarket() gas; assert it stays under block gas limit at maximum collateral count.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket / collateralParams for-loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they create a market with 128 collaterals trigger the call sequence market_creator calls touchMarket(128-collateral market) -> victim calls take() on that market for the first time (touchMarket is called inside take at Midnight.sol:347), violating the invariant that take() must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the first take on a 128-collateral market is significantly more expensive than subsequent takes due to the touchMarket initialization loop? Proof idea: measure gas delta between first and second take() on a 128-collateral market; assert the delta is bounded and does not cause OOG.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall / unbounded calls loop] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they construct a calls[] array of arbitrary length trigger the call sequence attacker calls multicall(calls) with calls.length set to a value that causes the for-loop at Midnight.sol:212 to consume the entire block gas, violating the invariant that multicall must not enable low-cost DoS of the contract, causing scoped impact: block-level DoS where a single multicall transaction consumes all block gas preventing other users from interacting with the protocol in the same block? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to 10000 with no-op delegatecalls; measure gas and assert that the loop overhead alone cannot fill a block.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall / unbounded calls loop with gate checks] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that a gated market exists with a moderately expensive IEnterGate trigger the call sequence attacker calls multicall([take1, take2, ..., takeN]) where each take increases credit and triggers canIncreaseCredit, violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify per-call gas costs into a block-filling attack, causing scoped impact: multiplicative gas griefing where N takes each paying gate-call overhead combine to exhaust block gas? Proof idea: fuzz N and gate gas cost; assert total multicall gas = N * (take_base_gas + gate_gas) stays under block limit for all valid N.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan / unbounded tokens loop] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they pass a tokens[] array of arbitrary length trigger the call sequence attacker calls flashLoan(tokens, assets, callback, data) with tokens.length set to a large value, violating the invariant that flashLoan must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the two for-loops at Midnight.sol:742-744 and 749-751 each iterate over the full tokens array making flashLoan a block-gas-consuming vector? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 1000; assert total flashLoan gas stays under block limit and that no single call can fill a block.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take / IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit + isHealthy while-loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy a market with an expensive IEnterGate and a borrower with 16 activated collaterals with expensive oracles trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys expensive gate -> borrower activates 16 collaterals -> taker calls take() on a buy offer, violating the invariant that take() must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: compounded gas griefing where gate overhead (canIncreaseCredit) plus 16-oracle health check loop at Midnight.sol:476 combine to make take() unexecutable? Proof idea: measure take() gas with gate_cost=X and oracle_count=N; assert gate_cost + N*oracle_cost + base_take_cost < block_gas_limit for all valid N.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidatorGate.canLiquidate + collateralBitmap while-loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy a market with an expensive ILiquidatorGate and a borrower with 16 activated collaterals with expensive oracles trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys expensive liquidator gate -> borrower activates 16 collaterals -> borrower becomes unhealthy -> liquidator calls liquidate(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: compounded gas griefing where gate overhead (canLiquidate at Midnight.sol:598) plus 16-oracle bitmap loop at Midnight.sol:607-618 combine to make liquidation unexecutable? Proof idea: fuzz gate_gas and oracle_gas independently; assert their sum plus base liquidate gas stays under block limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: withdrawCollateral / isHealthy while-loop] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate 16 collaterals with expensive oracles and have non-zero debt trigger the call sequence borrower calls withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf, receiver) which calls isHealthy at Midnight.sol:568, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a borrower with 16 expensive-oracle collaterals makes their own withdrawCollateral prohibitively expensive, effectively self-freezing their collateral? Proof idea: fuzz oracle gas cost and collateral count; assert withdrawCollateral gas stays under block limit for all valid configurations.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / unbounded takes loop] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they pass a takes[] array of arbitrary length trigger the call sequence attacker calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral(targetUnits, maxBuyerAssets, taker, permit, takes, withdrawals, receiver, feePct, feeRecipient) with takes.length set to a large value, violating the invariant that bundle functions must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the for-loop at MidnightBundles.sol:71 iterates over all takes even if targetUnits is reached early, consuming excess gas? Proof idea: fuzz takes.length with all offers already consumed; assert loop terminates early via filledUnits < targetUnits condition and does not iterate beyond necessary.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral / unbounded collateralWithdrawals loop] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they pass a collateralWithdrawals[] array of arbitrary length trigger the call sequence attacker calls repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, onBehalf, permit, collateralWithdrawals, receiver, feePct, feeRecipient) with collateralWithdrawals.length set to a large value, violating the invariant that bundle functions must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the for-loop at MidnightBundles.sol:336-345 iterates over all withdrawals each calling withdrawCollateral which itself calls isHealthy with a bitmap loop? Proof idea: fuzz collateralWithdrawals.length and oracle gas cost; assert total gas stays under block limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / unbounded collateralSupplies loop] Can an unprivileged caller under the precondition that they pass a collateralSupplies[] array of arbitrary length trigger the call sequence attacker calls supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with collateralSupplies.length set to a large value, violating the invariant that bundle functions must complete within block gas, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where the for-loop at MidnightBundles.sol:134-140 iterates over all supplies each calling supplyCollateral and safeTransferFrom, making the bundle prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: fuzz collateralSupplies.length from 1 to 1000; assert total gas stays under block limit.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take / IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy an IEnterGate that returns false for all accounts trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys AlwaysFalseGate -> touchMarket(market with enterGate=AlwaysFalseGate) -> any taker attempts take() with buyerCreditIncrease > 0, violating the invariant that gate checks must not permanently freeze lender credit in a market, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where no lender can ever gain credit in the market, making all buy offers permanently unfillable? Proof idea: deploy AlwaysFalseGate; assert take() always reverts with BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit and that no alternative credit-increasing path exists.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take / IEnterGate.canIncreaseDebt] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy an IEnterGate that returns false for canIncreaseDebt trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys DebtBlockingGate -> touchMarket(market with enterGate=DebtBlockingGate) -> any seller attempts take() with sellerDebtIncrease > 0, violating the invariant that gate checks must not permanently freeze borrowing in a market, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where no borrower can ever increase debt, making all sell offers permanently unfillable while existing debt positions remain trapped? Proof idea: deploy DebtBlockingGate; assert take() always reverts with SellerGatedFromIncreasingDebt and that repay() still works (gate does not block exit).

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / ILiquidatorGate.canLiquidate] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy an ILiquidatorGate that returns false for all callers trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys AlwaysFalseLiquidatorGate -> touchMarket(market with liquidatorGate=AlwaysFalseLiquidatorGate) -> borrower becomes unhealthy -> any liquidator calls liquidate(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent liquidation DoS enabling unchecked bad debt accumulation and lender insolvency? Proof idea: deploy AlwaysFalseLiquidatorGate; assert liquidate() always reverts with LiquidatorGatedFromLiquidating and that no alternative liquidation path bypasses the gate check at Midnight.sol:597-600.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: take / IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit + canIncreaseDebt] Can an unprivileged market creator under the precondition that they deploy an IEnterGate that is initially permissive but becomes restrictive after a state change trigger the call sequence market_creator deploys StatefulGate -> touchMarket -> users enter positions -> gate owner changes gate state to block all entry -> existing borrowers cannot increase debt and lenders cannot increase credit, violating the invariant that gate state changes must not retroactively freeze existing positions, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where existing positions cannot be unwound via take because credit/debt increases are blocked? Proof idea: deploy StatefulGate with owner-controlled toggle; assert that after toggle, take() reverts for new positions but repay() and withdraw() still succeed.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy / collateralBitmap while-loop + IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate 16 collaterals where one oracle reverts trigger the call sequence borrower calls supplyCollateral() for 16 collaterals including one with a reverting oracle -> borrower takes a sell offer to create debt -> any caller calls isHealthy(market, id, borrower), violating the invariant that health checks must not permanently revert for positions with non-zero debt, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the borrower's position cannot be health-checked, making withdrawCollateral, take (for the seller), and liquidate all permanently revert? Proof idea: deploy RevertingOracle; assert isHealthy() reverts and that no path exists to remove the reverting collateral without first passing a health check.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / collateralBitmap while-loop + IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they activate 16 collaterals where one oracle reverts after the borrower becomes unhealthy trigger the call sequence borrower activates 16 collaterals -> one oracle starts reverting -> borrower becomes unhealthy -> liquidator calls liquidate(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: permanent liquidation DoS where the bitmap loop at Midnight.sol:607-618 reverts on the reverting oracle, preventing bad debt realization? Proof idea: deploy oracle that reverts after a timestamp; assert liquidate() reverts and that the bad debt accumulates without recourse.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall / unbounded calls loop + IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that a gated market exists with an IEnterGate that has a per-call gas cost of G trigger the call sequence attacker calls multicall([take_1, take_2, ..., take_N]) where each take_i increases credit and triggers canIncreaseCredit, violating the invariant that multicall must not enable amplified gate-call gas attacks, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where N * G gate overhead in a single multicall transaction can fill a block and prevent other users from transacting? Proof idea: fuzz N and G; assert N * (base_take_gas + G) < block_gas_limit for all valid N values that fit in a single transaction.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall / unbounded calls loop + liquidate bitmap loop] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that a market has many unhealthy borrowers each with 16 activated collaterals trigger the call sequence attacker calls multicall([liquidate_borrower_1, liquidate_borrower_2, ..., liquidate_borrower_N]) where each liquidate triggers a 16-oracle bitmap loop, violating the invariant that multicall must not enable block-filling attacks, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where N * 16 * oracle_gas in a single multicall fills a block? Proof idea: fuzz N and oracle_gas; assert total multicall gas stays under block limit.
